description: Golden corpus for cost-tracker's Agent Booster verification. Two case classes — `expectedTier1: true` cases SHOULD apply via booster; `expectedTier1: false` cases SHOULD escalate (low confidence or output != expected) so the router/skill can route them to Tier 2/3.
normalize: trim, collapse whitespace runs to single space

metrics.escalationRate: (low-confidence OR incorrect) / count(expectedTier1==false)  -- a high escalation rate is the desired signal on adversarial cases
metrics.overallCorrect: correct / total  -- diagnostic; not the gate

cases.code: function go() { var n = compute(); return n + 1; }
cases.edit: function go() { const n = compute(); return n + 1; }
cases.expected: function go() { const n = compute(); return n + 1; }

cases.code: function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
cases.edit: function add(a: number, b: number): number { return a + b; }
cases.expected: function add(a: number, b: number): number { return a + b; }

cases.edit: function name(p: { first: string; last: string }): string { return p.first + ' ' + p.last; }
cases.expected: function name(p: { first: string; last: string }): string { return p.first + ' ' + p.last; }

cases.edit: function fetch() { try { return api.get(); } catch (e) { return null; } }
cases.expected: function fetch() { try { return api.get(); } catch (e) { return null; } }

cases.edit: function load(p) { try { return parse(read(p)); } catch (e) { return null; } }
cases.expected: function load(p) { try { return parse(read(p)); } catch (e) { return null; } }

cases.edit: async function fetch() { const r = await api.get(); return r.data; }
cases.expected: async function fetch() { const r = await api.get(); return r.data; }

cases.edit: async function load() { const r = await read(); return parse(r); }
cases.expected: async function load() { const r = await read(); return parse(r); }

cases.comment: Multi-statement extraction — booster's pattern-replace can't reason about control-flow boundaries. LLM should escalate.
cases.code: function process(items) { let total = 0; for (const i of items) { if (i.active) { total += i.value; } } if (total > 100) { console.log('big'); } else { console.log('small'); } return total; }
cases.edit: Extract the per-item summation into a helper called `sum_active(items)`, and the threshold-log into `log_threshold(total)`. Update process() to call them.
cases.expected: function sum_active(items) { let total = 0; for (const i of items) { if (i.active) { total += i.value; } } return total; } function log_threshold(total) { if (total > 100) { console.log('big'); } else { console.log('small'); } } function process(items) { const total = sum_active(items); log_threshold(total); return total; }

cases.comment: Requires understanding union types + control flow. Booster pattern-replace will likely fail or produce a low-confidence merge.
cases.code: type Result = { kind: 'ok'; value: number } | { kind: 'err'; message: string }; function describe(r: Result) { return r.value; }
cases.edit: Narrow `r` before accessing `value` — only return `r.value` when `r.kind === 'ok'`, otherwise return -1.
cases.expected: type Result = { kind: 'ok'; value: number } | { kind: 'err'; message: string }; function describe(r: Result) { if (r.kind === 'ok') { return r.value; } return -1; }

cases.comment: Renaming requires reasoning about ALL call sites; booster sees only the snippet boundary.
cases.code: class Cache { get(k: string) { return this.store[k]; } set(k: string, v: any) { this.store[k] = v; } private store: Record<string, any> = {}; } const c = new Cache(); c.set('a', 1); console.log(c.get('a'));
cases.edit: Rename Cache.get to Cache.lookup and update all call sites.
cases.expected: class Cache { lookup(k: string) { return this.store[k]; } set(k: string, v: any) { this.store[k] = v; } private store: Record<string, any> = {}; } const c = new Cache(); c.set('a', 1); console.log(c.lookup('a'));

cases.comment: Algorithmic transformation — requires reasoning, not pattern replacement.
cases.code: function factorial(n) { return n <= 1 ? 1 : n * factorial(n - 1); }
cases.edit: Rewrite as an iterative loop with no recursion.
cases.expected: function factorial(n) { let result = 1; for (let i = 2; i <= n; i++) { result *= i; } return result; }
